edges.description: Weak or dysregulated connective-tissue support permits recurrent subluxations, dislocations, soft-tissue injury, and chronic pain.
edges.source: Unresolved ECM remodeling and joint-stability failure

edges.description: In vitro hEDS/HSD fibroblasts show a candidate pro-inflammatory myofibroblast-like phenotype mediated through alpha-v-beta-3 integrin/ILK/Snail1 signaling.
edges.source: Unresolved ECM remodeling and joint-stability failure

edges.description: Joint-stability failure in hEDS presents clinically as generalized joint hypermobility.
Joint instability predisposes to recurrent subluxations and dislocations.
Repeated joint and soft-tissue injury contributes to chronic pain beyond acute injury pain.
Joint instability and repeated injury produce joint pain.

nodes.description: Joint pain is a very frequent musculoskeletal manifestation in hEDS.

evidence.reference_title: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome, hypermobility type (Orphanet structured-database record)

nodes.description: Chronic pain is common and may be distinct from acute injury pain.

evidence.snippet: Chronic pain, distinct from that associated with acute injury, is common and often neuropathic in nature.

nodes.description: Joint instability can cause recurrent subluxations or dislocations.

evidence.snippet: Subluxations, dislocations, and soft tissue injury are common; they may occur spontaneously or with minimal trauma and can be acutely painful.

nodes.description: Generalized joint hypermobility is the central diagnostic musculoskeletal feature of hEDS.

evidence.snippet: Hypermobile Ehlers-Danlos syndrome (hEDS) is characterized by generalized joint hypermobility

nodes.description: Dysregulated connective-tissue support and recurrent soft-tissue injury manifest clinically as subluxations, dislocations, acute injury pain, and chronic pain.

evidence.snippet: Subluxations, dislocations, and soft tissue injury are common; they may occur spontaneously or with minimal trauma and can be acutely painful.
Chronic pain, distinct from that associated with acute injury, is common and often neuropathic in nature.

nodes.description: hEDS/HSD patient fibroblasts can acquire a migrating myofibroblast-like phenotype with alpha-smooth muscle actin stress fibers, cadherin-11, MMP9, and inflammatory mediator changes through alpha-v-beta-3 integrin/ILK/Snail1 signaling. This remains model evidence rather than a validated diagnostic biomarker.

evidence.snippet: Our in vitro studies confirmed the phenotypic conversion of hEDS/HSD fibroblasts into migrating myofibroblast-like cells
In hEDS and HSD cells, the αvβ3 integrin was shown to be involved in the fibroblast-to-myofibroblast transition by a transduction pathway involving ILK that signals to the transcription factor Snail1

nodes.description: hEDS lacks a validated monogenic cause. Current model evidence points to abnormal collagen/fibronectin extracellular-matrix organization, excessive matrix remodeling, and pro-inflammatory myofibroblast-like fibroblast changes as candidate mechanisms that may contribute to joint instability, chronic pain, fatigue, and multisystem symptoms.
nodes.id: Unresolved ECM remodeling and joint-stability failure

evidence.snippet: Currently, no underlying genetic, epigenetic, or metabolomic etiology has been identified for hEDS.
skin fibroblasts show a disorganization of the ECM like that observed in cells derived from the other EDS types.
